title: Forms - Making fields required based on conditional question answers
excerpt:
On my Form, I have a field that has conditions under it.  Here is the scenario.  In this case I want the 2 conditional questions to be required if the answer to the first question is ‘Yes’.  Is that possible? 
(Question)…

posts.author: dbouche1 (Deb Boucher)
posts.cookedHtml:
On my Form, I have a field that has conditions under it. Here is the scenario. In this case I want the 2 conditional questions to be required if the answer to the first question is ‘Yes’. Is that possible?

(Question) Does this project require additional funding?
(condition 1): If ‘Yes’; Please enter the Additional Amount requested?
(condition 2) If ‘Yes’; Please describe the reason for the additional funding amount

posts.author: einavpeleg (Einav Peleg)
posts.cookedHtml:
When a question is marked as required in the form builder it will be required only when it appears. In your case only when the condition is met.

Hope that helps

posts.author: paisleygenesis (Paisley Genesis)
posts.cookedHtml:
Deb Boucher:

On my Form, I have a field that has conditions under it. Here is the scenario. In this case I want the 2 conditional questions to be required if the answer to the first question is ‘Yes’. Is that possible?

(Question) Does this project require additional funding?
(condition 1): If ‘Yes’; Please enter the Additional Amount requested?
(condition 2) If ‘Yes’; Please describe the reason for the additional funding amount

Yes, it’s definitely possible to set up conditional logic in a form where certain questions become required based on the answer to a preceding question. This approach ensures that respondents provide relevant information only when necessary, streamlining the form-filling process. It’s a practical way to gather specific details tailored to the respondent’s situation.
